cache_miss_taxonomy.name: cache_miss_taxonomy
cache_miss_taxonomy.description: Exact, near-prefix, and divergent-prefix requests to force explicit cache-hit/miss reasons.
shared_context: Exact prefix block: same repo context and same system instruction.
Near prefix block: one metadata field changes while most tokens remain identical.
Divergent prefix block: early tokens differ so the prefix cache must reject reuse.
user_templates: Request {request_id}: classify this as exact, near, or divergent prefix reuse.
Request {request_id}: explain which cache miss reason should be recorded.

long_prompt_burst.name: long_prompt_burst
long_prompt_burst.description: Many concurrent long prompts to expose prefill monopolization and cache pressure.
shared_context: Long-context document shard: design notes, benchmark output, issue triage, and implementation constraints.
Repeated system instruction: answer precisely, preserve code behavior, cite metrics, avoid unsupported claims.
Apple UMA constraint: model memory, KV memory, and cached state all share the same physical memory budget.
user_templates: Request {request_id}: answer a narrow question from this large shared context in less than 10 bullets.
Request {request_id}: extract the highest-risk scheduler issue from the long context.
Request {request_id}: state whether this long prompt should block short interactive requests.

mixed_latency.name: mixed_latency
mixed_latency.description: One long prefill class mixed with short interactive requests to test starvation and fairness.
shared_context: Short chat context: user asks a small local coding question.
Long agent context: full repo summary, previous tool traces, benchmark instructions, and serving roadmap.
Fairness target: short requests should continue to receive decode turns while long prefills progress in chunks.
user_templates: Request {request_id}: give the next action only.
Request {request_id}: produce a concise status update.
Request {request_id}: explain whether chunked prefill would help this case.

shared_agent.name: shared_agent
shared_agent.description: Repeated coding-agent turns with a large shared system/repo prefix and small user deltas.
shared_context: Repository map: crates/hawking-core, crates/hawking-serve, tools/bench, tools/training, docs/plans.
Project policy: Apple-first serving, Metal-focused runtime work, no unsupported backend assumptions.
Agent memory: preserve existing user edits, measure before public claims, report P95 latency.
Serving goal: repeated turns should reuse prompt state rather than rebuilding the shared context.
user_templates: Turn {request_id}: inspect the serving plan and identify the next concrete implementation step.
Turn {request_id}: continue from the previous turn and explain what cache/state should be reused.
Turn {request_id}: summarize risks for this Apple-local long-context workload.

spec_decode_gate.name: spec_decode_gate
spec_decode_gate.description: Speculative decoding on/off matrix for Apple-local net-win gating.
shared_context: Spec decode gate: drafting must improve accepted tokens/sec and must not regress P95 latency.
Apple-local caveat: small fast models may not amortize draft overhead on Metal.
user_templates: Request {request_id}: continue this deterministic text with a concise technical explanation.
Request {request_id}: repeat the relevant constraint and finish the sentence.
